Error: [2mexpect([22m[31mreceived[39m[2m).[22mtoMatch[2m([22m[32mexpected[39m[2m)[22m

Expected pattern: [32m/### Step 4\.5[:\s]/[39m
Received string:  [31m"---[39m
[31mname: gen-test-scripts[39m
[31mdescription: Generate executable e2e test scripts from test cases. Profile-aware: reads active test profile from .forge/config.yaml to determine test framework, templates, and conventions.[39m
[31m---·[39m
[31m# Gen Test Scripts·[39m
[31mGenerate executable e2e test scripts from test cases.·[39m
[31m**Core principle**: Every generated `test()` must be independently runnable, repeatable, and have explicit assertions. Test cases are input; scripts are output.·[39m
[31m<HARD-GATE>[39m
[31mThis skill ONLY writes to `tests/e2e/features/<feature>/` (staging area). It does NOT execute tests (handled by `/run-e2e-tests`).·[39m
[31m**FORBIDDEN output paths** — agents MUST NOT write spec files to any of these, regardless of project convention:[39m
[31m- `tests/e2e/<feature>/` (directly under e2e root — this is a post-graduation location)[39m
[31m- `tests/e2e/<module>/` (any module directory — reserved for graduated scripts)[39m
[31m- Any path outside `tests/e2e/features/`·[39m
[31m**Why this matters**: The `features/` prefix is a staging area that enforces a two-phase flow:[39m
[31m```[39m
[31mPhase 1: /gen-test-scripts → writes to tests/e2e/features/<feature>/[39m
[31mPhase 2: /graduate-tests   → classifies by functional module → moves to tests/e2e/<module>/[39m
[31m```[39m
[31mBypassing the staging area skips functional module classification in `/graduate-tests`, scattering tests into wrong directories. Existing directories at `tests/e2e/<module>/` are POST-GRADUATION locations — do NOT copy that convention during generation.[39m
[31m</HARD-GATE>·[39m
[31m## Step 0: Resolve Profile·[39m
[31m1. **Resolve profile**: Run `forge profile` to get the active test profile(s). This reads `.forge/config.yaml`, falls back to project structure detection.[39m
[31m2. **On failure** (output shows `PROFILE: (none)`): ask the user to choose from known profiles (`web-playwright`, `go-test`, `maestro`, `java-junit`, `rust-test`, `pytest`). Run `forge profile set <name>` to persist their choice.[39m
[31m3. **Load profile manifest**: Run `forge profile get <profile-name> --manifest`.[39m
[31m4. **Load profile strategy**: Run `forge profile get <profile-name> --generate`.·[39m
[31mUse the loaded profile manifest and strategy for all subsequent steps.·[39m
[31m<HARD-RULE>[39m
[31mDo NOT silently default to any profile. If `forge profile` returns no result and the user cannot decide, abort the skill.[39m
[31m</HARD-RULE>·[39m
[31m## Prerequisites·[39m
[31mCheck previous stage artifacts. Abort and prompt user if missing:·[39m
[31m| Artifact | Missing prompt |[39m
[31m|----------|----------------|[39m
[31m| `docs/features/<slug>/testing/test-cases.md` | Run `/gen-test-cases` first |[39m
[31m| `docs/sitemap/sitemap.json` (only when profile has `web-ui` capability) | Run `/gen-sitemap` first |·[39m
[31m`<slug>` is the current feature name, obtained via `forge feature` command. `docs/sitemap/sitemap.json` is a project-level file (one per application), not isolated per feature.·[39m
[31m### Step Actionability Gate·[39m
[31mCheck whether an eval-test-cases report exists for the current feature. If it does, parse the Step Actionability score and enforce the blocking threshold.·[39m
[31m**Check procedure**:·[39m
[31m1. List evaluation reports: `ls docs/features/<slug>/testing/eval/iteration-*.md 2>/dev/null`[39m
[31m2. If no reports exist, proceed normally (backward compatible — eval is optional)[39m
[31m3. If reports exist, read the latest iteration report (highest N)[39m
[31m4. Find the Step Actionability dimension score in the report's dimension table[39m
[31m5. If Step Actionability < 200: **ABORT** with the message below·[39m
[31m**Abort message**:[39m
[31m```[39m
[31mABORT: Step Actionability score is below the blocking threshold (score < 200).·[39m
[31mThe test cases in test-cases.md have insufficient step-level detail for reliable[39m
[31mscript generation. Low Step Actionability means test steps are vague, missing[39m
[31mconcrete actions, or lack sufficient detail for automated translation.·[39m
[31mAction required: Run /eval-test-cases, review the Step Actionability dimension[39m
[31mfeedback, and fix test-cases.md to improve step-level specificity before[39m
[31mre-running /gen-test-scripts.[39m
[31m```·[39m
[31m<HARD-RULE>[39m
[31mIf eval-test-cases report exists and Step Actionability < 200, gen-test-scripts MUST abort. This gate prevents generating scripts from poorly-specified test cases, which historically leads to 3-5 rounds of agent-driven fixes. The threshold of 200 (out of 250) is the minimum for unambiguous step-to-script translation.[39m
[31m</HARD-RULE>·[39m
[31m### Sitemap (web-ui capability only)·[39m
[31mWhen the active profile has `web-ui` capability, the sitemap provides locator source for UI tests: `docs/sitemap/sitemap.json` (generated by `/gen-sitemap`, full example: `plugins/forge/references/shared/sitemap.json`).·[39m
[31m**Key locator fields**:·[39m
[31m| Field | Usage |[39m
[31m|-------|-------|[39m
[31m| `layout.elements[].role` + `name` | Layout-level shared elements (navbar, etc.), available for all pages wrapped by `layout.wraps` |[39m
[31m| `elements[].role` + `name` | Semantic role+name pair — translate to framework locator per `generate.md` |[39m
[31m| `elements[].level` | Heading level for precise targeting |[39m
[31m| `elements[].label` | Accessible label — translate to framework locator per `generate.md` |[39m
[31m| `elements[].placeholder` | Placeholder text — translate to framework locator per `generate.md` |[39m
[31m| `states[].trigger` | Click trigger element to enter dynamic state |[39m
[31m| `states[].elements` | Elements within dynamic states |·[39m
[31mTest cases match sitemap pages via the `Route` field. `Element` field is **required** in test-cases.md (E-NNN / L-NNN IDs). When present, provides precise mapping; when set to `sitemap-missing`, use Fact Table DOM structure from Step 1.5 Code Reconnaissance to infer locators — match test step descriptions to the most appropriate semantic locators based on actual code structure.·[39m
[31m**Handling `sitemap-missing` Element values**: When a test case has `Element: sitemap-missing`, the sitemap is unavailable for that route. Fall back to the Fact Table built in Step 1.5:[39m
[31m1. Read the actual page component source code[39m
[31m2. Extract semantic roles, labels, and test IDs from the DOM structure[39m
[31m3. Generate locators following the priority system in `generate.md`[39m
[31m4. Add a `// VERIFY: sitemap-missing — locator inferred from source code` comment on each inferred locator·[39m
[31m## When to Use·[39m
[31m**Trigger:**·[39m
[31m- User asks to \"generate test scripts\" or \"create e2e scripts\"[39m
[31m- User provides `/gen-test-scripts` command[39m
[31m- After `/gen-test-cases` has produced `testing/test-cases.md`·[39m
[31m**Skip:**[39m
[31m- `tests/e2e/features/<slug>/` already exists and contains generated scripts (re-run only if explicitly requested)·[39m
[31m## Workflow·[39m
[31m<HARD-RULE>[39m
[31m**Task Splitting Guard**: When gen-test-scripts is split into parallel sub-tasks (e.g., by output file or test case range), the following MUST hold:·[39m
[31m1. **Global Setup Phase** (Steps 0→1→1.5→3.5) runs as a single pre-task FIRST. This produces:[39m
[31m   - Auth Plan (from Step 1 classification)[39m
[31m   - Fact Table (from Step 1.5 reconnaissance)[39m
[31m   - Shared infrastructure (from Step 3.5: auth-setup.ts, playwright.config.ts, helpers.ts, config files)[39m
[31m2. **Only Step 4** (spec file generation) may be parallelized into sub-tasks, each handling a subset of test cases[39m
[31m3. Each sub-task's description MUST include: \"Shared infrastructure already configured in pre-task. Auth: use storageState (Playwright) / cached token (API), NOT login() in beforeEach.\"·[39m
[31m**Why**: Steps 0-3.5 produce shared artifacts that ALL spec files depend on. Splitting them causes each subagent to skip shared infrastructure and fall back to per-test login (see `docs/lessons/gotcha-split-task-missing-shared-setup.md`).[39m
[31m</HARD-RULE>·[39m
[31m```[39m
[31m0. Resolve profile → 1. Read test cases → 1.5. Code Reconnaissance → 2. Resolve sitemap (web-ui) → 3. Map locators (web-ui) → 3.5. Ensure shared infrastructure (auth + helpers + config) → 4. Generate spec files[39m
[31m```·[39m
[31m### Step 1: Read Test Cases·[39m
[31mRead `docs/features/<slug>/testing/test-cases.md`. Parse each test case — extract TC ID, title, type, route, feature, pre-conditions, steps, expected result, priority. Group by type using the profile's capabilities (e.g., `web-ui` → UI, `api` → API, `cli` → CLI).·[39m
[31m#### Auth Classification·[39m
[31mFor each test case, classify by authentication requirements:·[39m
[31m| Category | Detection Rules | Generation Strategy |[39m
[31m|----------|----------------|---------------------|[39m
[31m| **login-test** | `Target` matches auth/login routes (e.g., `ui/login`, `api/auth`, `api/token`) | No shared auth. Generate independent login/logout logic. Must invalidate cached credentials after to prevent stale state. |[39m
[31m| **auth-required-test** | `Pre-conditions` contain \"authenticated\", \"logged in\", or `Target` implies protected resource | Use **cached shared auth** — credentials acquired once, reused across all tests |[39m
[31m| **public-test** | No auth-related pre-conditions, target is a public resource | No auth needed |[39m
[31m| **custom-auth-test** | `Pre-conditions` mention \"API key\", \"X-API-Key\", \"OAuth\", \"session cookie\", or other non-Bearer auth patterns | Detect auth mechanism from codebase during Step 1.5. Generate custom auth setup |·[39m
[31m**Classification priority** (evaluated top-down, first match wins):[39m
[31m1. `custom-auth-test` — takes priority over `auth-required-test` when both match (e.g., \"authenticated via API key\" → custom-auth, not auth-required)[39m
[31m2. `login-test`[39m
[31m3. `auth-required-test`[39m
[31m4. `public-test`·[39m
[31mCount each category to decide whether to enable shared auth (enabled when `auth-required-test` exists).·[39m
[31m**Auth Plan Output**: Record the classification result. This is the contract consumed by Step 3.5 (auth infrastructure) and Step 4 (spec generation):[39m
[31m```[39m
[31mAuth Plan:[39m
[31m  auth-required-test: N (routes: ...)[39m
[31m  login-test: N (routes: ...)[39m
[31m  public-test: N[39m
[31m  custom-auth-test: N (mechanism: ...)[39m
[31m  shared-auth-enabled: yes/no[39m
[31m```·[39m
[31m**Credential caching**: Follow the rules in the active profile's `generate.md` for framework-specific credential caching (e.g., storageState for browser-based UI, cached tokens for API). Login tests must invalidate cached credentials after completion.·[39m
[31m<HARD-RULE>[39m
[31mLogin tests and authenticated tests must not be mixed in the same `describe` block.[39m
[31m</HARD-RULE>·[39m
[31m### Step 1.5: Code Reconnaissance (Build Fact Table)·[39m
[31mRead actual source code files to extract ground-truth values. **Never guess or assume values** — every value in a generated script must come from the test-cases.md input or the Fact Table built here.·[39m
[31m**Check test-cases.md** for Route Validation results (warnings from gen-test-cases Step 3.5). Use corrected routes where available.·[39m
[31m**Required reads** (adapt to project structure):·[39m
[31m| Source | What to extract | Discovery guidance |[39m
[31m|--------|----------------|---------------------|[39m
[31m| Router files | Route paths, path parameters, middleware bindings | Search for route registration patterns, configuration files, and entry points using Grep. Look for URL path strings, HTTP method bindings, and path parameter definitions. |[39m
[31m| Config files | API port, base path prefix, auth credentials | Search for config/settings files (`.env`, `config.*`, `settings.*`). Look for port numbers, base URLs, and credential variable names. |[39m
[31m| API handlers | Request/response schemas, status codes, validation rules | Search for request handler functions and response definitions. Look for status code usage, input validation, and response body shaping. |[39m
[31m| Auth implementation | Login endpoint path, token field name, header format | Search for authentication/authorization modules. Look for login endpoints, token generation/parsing, and header middleware. |[39m
[31m| CLI entry points | Command names, flag names, output formats | Search for command registration and argument parsing. Look for CLI framework usage (e.g., cobra, commander, click) and output formatting. |·[39m
[31m**Build the Fact Table**: After reading, record verified facts with source citations:·[39m
[31m```markdown[39m
[31m## Fact Table[39m
[31m| Key | Value | Source |[39m
[31m|-----|-------|--------|[39m
[31m| API_PORT | 8080 | backend/config.yaml:3 |[39m
[31m| AUTH_ENDPOINT | POST /v1/auth/login | internal/handler/router.go:42 |[39m
[31m```·[39m
[31m<HARD-RULE>[39m
[31m- Every value in the Fact Table must cite the source file and line number.[39m
[31m- If a source file cannot be found, note it as `UNKNOWN`. Do not fabricate values.[39m
[31m- The agent must use Fact Table values when generating spec files in Step 4. When a Fact Table value contradicts a template placeholder, the Fact Table wins.[39m
[31m- All `// VERIFY:` markers in templates must be resolved using Fact Table values.[39m
[31m</HARD-RULE>·[39m
[31m### Step 2: Resolve Sitemap (web-ui capability only)·[39m
[31m**Only execute when the active profile has `web-ui` capability AND UI-type test cases exist.**·[39m
[31mRead `docs/sitemap/sitemap.json`. For each route referenced in test cases:·[39m
[31m1. Match `Route` field to `sitemap.json` `pages[].route` (dynamic routes like `/tasks/:id` match specific paths like `/tasks/123`)[39m
[31m2. Collect all element data for matched pages (base elements + related states)[39m
[31m3. **If test case has `Element` field with E-NNN / L-NNN IDs**: use only specified elements for precise mapping[39m
[31m4. **If test case has `Element: sitemap-missing`**: skip sitemap lookup for this test case; use Fact Table DOM structure from Step 1.5 to infer locators in Step 3[39m
[31m5. **If sitemap has `layout` field**: for each route wrapped by `layout.wraps`, merge `layout.elements` as available elements·[39m
[31mIf a route referenced in test cases does not exist in sitemap, report the missing route and suggest re-running `/gen-sitemap`.·[39m
[31m### Step 3: Map Locators (web-ui capability only)·[39m
[31m**Only execute when the active profile has `web-ui` capability.**·[39m
[31mFollow the locator mapping rules in the active profile's `generate.md` to translate sitemap element data into framework-specific locator code. The priority order (role > label > placeholder > text > testid) and syntax are defined per framework in `generate.md`.·[39m
[31mFor test steps within dynamic states: first click the trigger element's locator, then map locators for in-state elements. Build an in-memory mapping table for use in Step 4.·[39m
[31m### Step 3.5: Ensure Shared Infrastructure·[39m
[31m<PRINCIPLE>[39m
[31m**Shared infrastructure first.** Before generating any test files, ensure that shared dependencies are complete and functional. Test files depend on these shared files via import — if they are missing or incomplete, all tests will fail at the import stage. Downstream skills (`/run-e2e-tests`, `/graduate-tests`) follow the same principle.[39m
[31m</PRINCIPLE>·[39m
[31mCheck if shared infrastructure exists at `tests/e2e/`:·[39m
[31m```bash[39m
[31mls tests/e2e/ 2>/dev/null[39m
[31m```·[39m
[31m**If any shared file is missing**, create it from the corresponding template (paths from profile manifest `templates.*`):·[39m
[31m- Helper file: copy from `{profile-templates-dir}/{manifest.templates.helpers}` (only if missing). When copying, strip all `// VERIFY:` and `// TEMPLATE:` comments — these are generation-time markers that should not appear in runtime files.[39m
[31m- Config files: copy from manifest template references (only if missing)[39m
[31m- Follow `generate.md` for additional shared infrastructure requirements specific to the framework·[39m
[31m**If helper file already exists**: check whether it exports all symbols required by the Auth Plan (e.g., `ensureAuthState`, `getApiToken`). If symbols are missing, add the missing exports AND all their private dependencies from the template. Do NOT overwrite existing exports — merge.·[39m
[31m#### Auth Infrastructure·[39m
[31mBased on the Auth Plan from Step 1, configure auth infrastructure for auth-required tests.·[39m
[31m**When `shared-auth-enabled: yes`** (auth-required-test count > 0):·[39m
[31m1. Follow the active profile's `generate.md` for framework-specific auth setup[39m
[31m2. For playwright profile:[39m
[31m   - Generate `tests/e2e/auth-setup.ts` from template (if not already present)[39m
[31m   - Configure `tests/e2e/playwright.config.ts`: uncomment the `projects` section with `setup` + `authenticated` projects using `storageState`[39m
[31m3. For other profiles: follow `generate.md` auth setup instructions[39m
[31m4. Verify `helpers.ts` exports all auth-related symbols needed by spec files·[39m
[31m**When `shared-auth-enabled: no`**: Skip auth infrastructure setup.·[39m
[31m<HARD-RULE>[39m
[31mAuth-required tests MUST use the shared auth mechanism (e.g., storageState for Playwright, cached tokens for API), NOT per-test login in beforeEach. The auth infrastructure generated here provides shared credentials — spec files in Step 4 MUST reference it.·[39m
[31mSpecifically for Playwright: when the `projects` section in playwright.config.ts is configured with `storageState`, calling `login()` or `loginViaUI()` in `beforeEach` is FORBIDDEN for auth-required tests.[39m
[31m</HARD-RULE>·[39m
[31m<HARD-RULE>[39m
[31m**Shared file policy**: Shared files at `tests/e2e/` are shared across all features.[39m
[31m- If they do not exist: create them from templates.[39m
[31m- If helper file exists: merge missing exports from template into it (do NOT overwrite existing exports).[39m
[31m- Other shared files (config, package manifest, etc.): if they exist, DO NOT modify.[39m
[31m- Only test files are written per-feature.[39m
[31m</HARD-RULE>·[39m
[31m### Step 4: Generate Spec Files·[39m
[31m**Verify project interfaces before generating**: For each type group from Step 1, confirm the project actually exposes that interface. Build/test/lint commands are developer tooling, not a CLI product interface.·[39m
[31m| Type | Probe command | Evidence of product interface |[39m
[31m|------|--------------|-------------------------------|[39m
[31m| UI | `ls docs/sitemap/sitemap.json` or `grep -r \"router\\|<Route\\|page\\.\" src/` | Sitemap exists, or frontend route registration found |[39m
[31m| API | `grep -rn \"router\\|handler\\|endpoint\\|HandleFunc\\|app.get\\|app.post\" --include='*.go' --include='*.ts' --include='*.js' .` | HTTP handler registration patterns found |[39m
[31m| CLI | `grep '\"bin\"' package.json` or `ls cmd/` or `grep -rn \"cobra.Command\" --include='*.go' .` | CLI entry point or command framework detected |·[39m
[31mFor each non-empty, verified type group, generate a test file from the corresponding template in the profile's `templates/` directory (paths from `manifest.templates.*`):·[39m
[31m- UI tests: `{profile-templates-dir}/{manifest.templates.test-file-ui}`[39m
[31m- API tests: `{profile-templates-dir}/{manifest.templates.test-file-api}`[39m
[31m- CLI tests: `{profile-templates-dir}/{manifest.templates.test-file-cli}`·[39m
[31m**Follow the active profile's `generate.md`** for all framework-specific patterns:[39m
[31m- Test runner imports, annotations, and lifecycle hooks[39m
[31m- Assertion library usage and conventions[39m
[31m- HTTP client for API tests[39m
[31m- Process execution for CLI tests[39m
[31m- Auth credential injection mechanism[39m
[31m- Import path conventions (staging: relative path to shared helpers)[39m
[31m- Test class/file naming conventions·[39m
[31mBased on Step 1 auth classification, uncomment matching CONDITIONAL blocks in templates, remove non-matching blocks, then fill in test data. Replace example content with actual test cases from `test-cases.md`, keeping the same structure. Do not rewrite template structure from scratch.·[39m
[31m#### Integration Test Scripts·[39m
[31mIntegration test cases verify that a component embedded on an existing page is visible and renders correctly. Follow the profile's `generate.md` for framework-specific patterns.·[39m
[31m**Identification**: A test case is an integration test when:[39m
[31m- `Source` field contains \"Placement + Integration Spec\", **or**[39m
[31m- `Test ID` field contains \"integration-\"·[39m
[31mIntegration tests use the same framework and file structure as standard tests. They are grouped with UI test cases and emitted into the same test file.·[39m
[31m**Script generation strategy** — for each integration test case:·[39m
[31m1. **Locate the target page** by route (using sitemap resolution from Step 2)[39m
[31m2. **Locate the embedded component** using the locator strategy from `generate.md`[39m
[31m3. **Assert visibility**: use framework-specific visibility assertion[39m
[31m4. **Assert position** (if feasible): verify the component appears at expected position[39m
[31m5. **Verify data rendering**: assert expected text content is present within the component locator·[39m
[31m#### beforeAll Safety·[39m
[31mAll setup blocks (`beforeAll`, `@BeforeAll`, `setUp`, etc.) must follow defensive patterns to prevent cascade failures where module-level variables stay uninitialized:·[39m
[31m1. **Wrap each operation in try/catch** with error logging identifying which step failed[39m
[31m2. **Explicit null/undefined/None check** — after assignment, validate the value is set[39m
[31m3. **Prefer reusing existing resources** over creating new ones — fewer operations means fewer failure modes[39m
[31m4. **Use sequential execution** when tests have dependencies, instead of shared mutable state[39m
[31m5. **Use retry mechanisms** for backend-dependent operations (token acquisition, resource creation) to handle transient failures·[39m
[31m<HARD-RULE>[39m
[31mWhen setup blocks throw and module-level variables stay uninitialized, all downstream tests fail with misleading errors (e.g., `undefined` in URL paths → 404). The try/catch + explicit check pattern ensures the *real* failure is reported, not a secondary symptom.[39m
[31m</HARD-RULE>·[39m
[31m#### Anti-Patterns (Forbidden in Generated Code)·[39m
[31mFollow the anti-pattern rules in the active profile's `generate.md` strategy. Common anti-patterns across all profiles:·[39m
[31m<HARD-RULE>[39m
[31m**No fixed delays/sleeps.** These mask real timing issues, waste time when the app is fast, and fail when the app is slow. Every wait must be event-driven or use polling with timeout.·[39m
[31m**No hardcoded URLs.** Use config/environment variables for all endpoint URLs.·[39m
[31m**No debug output in generated code.** Only assertions — no print/console.log for debugging.·[39m
[31m**Selector strategy**: Follow `generate.md` for selector priority. Never bind to internal implementation details (CSS classes, DOM structure) when semantic selectors are available.[39m
[31m</HARD-RULE>·[39m
[31m<EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>[39m
[31mAll framework-specific rules (test runner, assertion library, imports, HTTP client, process execution, anti-patterns) are defined in the active profile's `generate.md` strategy file (loaded in Step 0). Read and follow those rules precisely.·[39m
[31m- Use ONLY the framework specified in the profile's `generate.md`[39m
[31m- **`@eN` refs must not appear in any generated test file.**[39m
[31m</EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>·[39m
[31m<HARD-RULE>[39m
[31m**Traceability**: Each test function must include a traceability comment ensuring complete traceability chain from test script to PRD.·[39m
[31m**Skip empty groups**: If no test cases of a given type exist, or the project lacks that interface (Step 4 verification), skip generating that test file.·[39m
[31m**Empty result guard**: If zero test files were generated (all groups empty or all interfaces undetected), abort with a clear message: \"No test files generated — either test-cases.md has no testable cases, or all interface types were undetected. Re-run /gen-test-cases or verify project structure.\"·[39m
[31m**VERIFY marker resolution**: Resolve all `// VERIFY:` comments using Fact Table values. If no Fact Table value exists, keep the `// VERIFY:` comment as-is.·[39m
[31m**Post-generation compilation check**: Run `just e2e-compile` to verify generated files compile. Fix any compilation errors before completing.·[39m
[31m**Post-generation VERIFY check**: Verify no unresolved `// VERIFY:` markers remain:[39m
[31m1. Run `just e2e-verify --feature <slug>` if the recipe exists in the Justfile[39m
[31m2. Otherwise: `grep -rn '// VERIFY:' tests/e2e/features/<slug>/` (adapt file extension to profile)·[39m
[31m**Post-generation helper merge**: After generating all spec files, verify `helpers.ts` exports cover all imports used by generated specs. If any import is missing from helpers, merge it from the template (do NOT overwrite existing exports).[39m
[31m</HARD-RULE>·[39m
[31m## Output·[39m
[31mAll generated test files go to `tests/e2e/features/<feature>/` (staging area). After verification via `/run-e2e-tests`, use `/graduate-tests` to migrate them to the regression suite. Output file names are determined by the profile's manifest template mappings.·[39m
[31m## Error Handling·[39m
[31m| Situation | Action |[39m
[31m|-----------|--------|[39m

[31m**No hardcoded URLs.** Use config/environment variables for all endpoint URLs.·[39m
[31m**No debug output in generated code.** Only assertions — no print/console.log for debugging.·[39m
[31m**Selector strategy**: Follow `generate.md` for selector priority. Never bind to internal implementation details (CSS classes, DOM structure) when semantic selectors are available.[39m
[31m</HARD-RULE>·[39m
[31m<EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>[39m

[31m**Traceability**: Each test function must include a traceability comment ensuring complete traceability chain from test script to PRD.·[39m
[31m**Skip empty groups**: If no test cases of a given type exist, or the project lacks that interface (Step 4 verification), skip generating that test file.·[39m
[31m**Empty result guard**: If zero test files were generated (all groups empty or all interfaces undetected), abort with a clear message: \"No test files generated — either test-cases.md has no testable cases, or all interface types were undetected. Error: [2mexpect([22m[31mreceived[39m[2m).[22mtoMatch[2m([22m[32mexpected[39m[2m)[22m

Expected pattern: [32m/eval-cases.*main.session|MAIN_SESSION.*eval-cases/i[39m
Received string:  [31m"---[39m
[31mname: run-tasks[39m
[31mdescription: Autonomous task dispatcher that continuously claims tasks and dispatches to subagents.[39m
[31mallowed_tools: [\"Bash\", \"Read\", \"Agent\", \"TaskOutput\", \"Skill\"][39m
[31m---·[39m
[31m# /run-tasks·[39m
[31mAuto-dispatch tasks. MAIN_SESSION tasks execute in main session; all others dispatch to forge:task-executor subagent (which calls `forge prompt get-by-task-id` internally).·[39m
[31m## Architecture·[39m
[31m```mermaid[39m
[31mflowchart TD[39m
[31m    A[\"1. Claim Task\"] --> B{\"MAIN_SESSION?\"}[39m
[31m    B -->|\"yes\"| C[\"1.5 Follow Task Instructions\"][39m
[31m    C --> LOOP([\"Step 4: Continue Loop\"])[39m
[31m    B -->|\"no\"| D[\"2. Dispatch + Verify\"][39m
[31m    D --> E[\"3. Breaking Gate\"][39m
[31m    E --> LOOP[39m
[31m    LOOP --> A[39m
[31m```·[39m
[31m## Dispatcher Iron Laws·[39m
[31m<EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>[39m
[31m1. Only 4 actions: claim → (main_session? follow task instructions : dispatch+verify) → breaking gate[39m
[31m2. NO code reading, NO code writing — EXCEPT for MAIN_SESSION tasks (Step 1.5) where the Skill tool is invoked in the main session[39m
[31m3. NO running tests directly — EXCEPT in Step 3 (Breaking Task Gate) where `just test` and `just test-e2e` are executed as quality gates[39m
[31m4. 30-minute timeout per task[39m
[31m5. 3 consecutive failures → STOP[39m
[31m</EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>·[39m
[31m## Execution Loop·[39m
[31m### Step 1: Claim Task·[39m
[31m```bash[39m
[31mforge task claim[39m
[31m```·[39m
[31m**Output parsing**:[39m
[31m- `ACTION: CLAIMED` → New task[39m
[31m- `ACTION: CONTINUE` → Resume existing task[39m
[31m- Error → No available task, end loop·[39m
[31m**Extract from claim output**:[39m
[31m- `TASK_ID` (e.g., \"2.1\")[39m
[31m- `KEY` (e.g., \"2.1-implementation\")[39m
[31m- `FILE` (e.g., full absolute path to task file)[39m
[31m- `BREAKING` (e.g., \"true\" or absent)[39m
[31m- `MAIN_SESSION` (e.g., \"true\" or absent)[39m
[31m- `SCOPE` (e.g., \"frontend\", \"backend\", or \"all\" — defaults to \"all\" if absent; may be omitted entirely by claim output when not set)[39m
[31m- `FEATURE` (e.g., \"my-feature\" — feature slug from claim output)·[39m
[31m### Step 1.5: Main Session Routing·[39m
[31mIf `MAIN_SESSION == \"true\"`:·[39m
[31m1. Read the task file at the FILE path extracted from claim output and find the `## Main Session Instructions` section.[39m
[31m2. Follow the instructions exactly — the task document specifies what skill to invoke, how to check outcome, and how to record the result.[39m
[31m3. The dispatcher does NOT hardcode skill names or record logic — it delegates to the task document.[39m
[31m4. If the task file lacks a `## Main Session Instructions` section, mark the task blocked and report: \"MAIN_SESSION task missing Main Session Instructions section — task document is incomplete\".[39m
[31m5. After execution, verify the record file exists via `forge task query <TASK_ID>`. If STATUS is not `\"completed\"`, spawn fix task (same as Step 2 verify logic).[39m
[31m6. Skip to Step 4 (Continue Loop).·[39m
[31mElse:[39m
[31m- Proceed to Step 2 (Dispatch + Verify).·[39m
[31m### Step 2: Dispatch + Verify·[39m
[31m#### 2a. Dispatch·[39m
[31m```[39m
[31mAgent([39m
[31m  subagent_type=\"forge:task-executor\",[39m
[31m  prompt=\"Execute task <TASK_ID>\"[39m
[31m)[39m
[31m```·[39m
[31mThe subagent internally runs `forge prompt get-by-task-id <TASK_ID>` to get the execution strategy.·[39m
[31m**Timeout**: 30 minutes·[39m
[31m#### 2b. Verify Record·[39m
[31mAfter subagent returns, check the task's actual status via CLI:·[39m
[31m```bash[39m
[31mforge task query <TASK_ID>[39m
[31m```·[39m
[31m- **STATUS == `\"completed\"`**: proceed to Step 3 (Breaking Gate).[39m
[31m- **STATUS != `\"completed\"`**: task was auto-downgraded (e.g. test failures).[39m
[31m  **Auto-downgrade rule**: If `testsFailed > 0`, `forge task submit` automatically downgrades `completed` to `blocked` (non-overridable, even with `--force`). All tests must pass for completion.[39m
[31m  Spawn fix task using `--block-source` to atomically block the source:[39m
[31m  ```bash[39m
[31m  forge task add --template fix-task --title \"Fix: <failure>\" \\[39m
[31m    --source-task-id <TASK_ID> \\[39m
[31m    --block-source \\[39m
[31m    --description \"<reason>\"[39m
[31m  ```[39m
[31m  `forge task add` automatically deduplicates — check output:[39m
[31m  - `ACTION: ADDED` → new fix task created, continue loop[39m
[31m  - `ACTION: SKIPPED` → active fix task already exists, continue loop·[39m
[31m#### 2c. Record-Missing Recovery·[39m
[31mWhen the subagent completes but the record file is missing, delegate recovery to another subagent:·[39m
[31m```[39m
[31mAgent([39m
[31m  subagent_type=\"forge:task-executor\",[39m
[31m  prompt=\"Fix record for task <TASK_ID>\"[39m
[31m)[39m
[31m```·[39m
[31mThe subagent's Execution Protocol detects the \"Fix record for\" prefix and calls `forge prompt get-by-task-id <TASK_ID> --fix-record-missed` internally.·[39m
[31m### Step 3: Breaking Task Gate·[39m
[31mDetermine which gates to run based on claim output from Step 1:·[39m
[31m| BREAKING=true? | SCOPE frontend\\|all + specs exist? | Run 3a? | Run 3b? |[39m
[31m|----------------|-------------------------------------|---------|---------|[39m
[31m| Yes | No | Yes | No |[39m
[31m| No | Yes | No | Yes |[39m
[31m| Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes |[39m
[31m| No | No | Skip Step 3 entirely | Skip Step 3 entirely |·[39m
[31mIf running both: execute 3a first. Only proceed to 3b if 3a passes.·[39m
[31m#### 3a. Unit/Integration Gate (BREAKING: true)·[39m
[31m```bash[39m
[31m# Pre-flight: verify justfile and test recipe exist[39m
[31mif [ ! -f justfile ] && [ ! -f Justfile ]; then[39m
[31m    echo \"Error: justfile not found — run /init-justfile first\" >&2[39m
[31m    exit 1[39m
[31mfi[39m
[31mjust --list 2>/dev/null | grep -q \"^    test \" || {[39m
[31m    echo \"Error: 'test' recipe not found in justfile\" >&2[39m
[31m    exit 1[39m
[31m}[39m
[31m```·[39m
[31m```bash[39m
[31mjust test [scope][39m
[31m```·[39m
[31mApply the **Scope Resolution** protocol from the Forge Guide — use the `SCOPE` extracted from the claim output in Step 1.·[39m
[31m**If tests fail**:[39m
[31m- Add fix task:[39m
[31m  ```bash[39m
[31m  forge task add --template fix-task --title \"Fix: <failure>\" \\[39m
[31m    --source-task-id <TASK_ID> \\[39m
[31m    --block-source \\[39m
[31m    --var SOURCE_FILES=\"<affected paths>\" \\[39m
[31m    --var TEST_SCRIPT=\"<failing test>\" \\[39m
[31m    --var TEST_RESULTS=\"<results path>\" \\[39m
[31m    --description \"<root cause>\"[39m
[31m  ```[39m
[31m  **`--block-source`**: atomically sets source task to blocked before resolution, preserving the fix-chain model.[39m
[31m  **`--source-task-id` auto-resolves**: if `<TASK_ID>` is a **completed** fix-task, the CLI automatically resolves to the root blocked task. Always pass the current failing task's ID — no manual chain tracing needed.[39m
[31m- Continue loop — fix task (P0) will be claimed on next iteration[39m
[31m- Do NOT proceed to next task until fix task completes·[39m
[31m**If tests pass**: if the routing table indicates 3b should also run (SCOPE frontend|all + specs exist), proceed to 3b. Otherwise continue to next iteration (Step 1).·[39m
[31m#### 3b. Feature E2E Gate (SCOPE=frontend|all, specs exist)·[39m
[31m<EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>[39m
[31mThe dispatcher evaluates SCOPE and FEATURE from Step 1 claim output BEFORE executing any bash commands below. If SCOPE is `backend` or FEATURE is empty, skip this entire section.[39m
[31m</EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>·[39m
[31mPre-conditions (all must be true):[39m
[31m- SCOPE is `frontend` or `all` (defaults to \"all\" if absent from claim output)[39m
[31m- FEATURE is non-empty (always true after successful claim)[39m
[31m- Feature has e2e spec files: `tests/e2e/features/$FEATURE/` directory is non-empty[39m
[31m- `test-e2e` recipe exists in justfile·[39m
[31m```bash[39m
[31m# Pre-flight: verify test-e2e recipe exists — if missing, skip to next iteration[39m
[31mSKIP=\"\"[39m
[31mjust --list 2>/dev/null | grep -q \"test-e2e\" || { echo \"Skip: test-e2e recipe not found\"; SKIP=true; }·[39m
[31m# Check if specs exist for this feature[39m
[31mif [ ! -d \"tests/e2e/features/$FEATURE/\" ] || [ -z \"$(ls -A \"tests/e2e/features/$FEATURE/\" 2>/dev/null)\" ]; then[39m
[31m    echo \"Skip: no spec files in tests/e2e/features/$FEATURE/\"[39m
[31m    SKIP=true[39m
[31mfi·[39m
[31m# If pre-flights passed, run e2e[39m
[31mif [ -z \"$SKIP\" ]; then[39m
[31m    just e2e-setup[39m
[31m    just test-e2e --feature \"$FEATURE\"[39m
[31mfi[39m
[31m```·[39m
[31m**If e2e fails**:[39m
[31m- Add fix task using the fix-task template:[39m
[31m  ```bash[39m
[31m  forge task add --template fix-task --title \"Fix: <concise description>\" \\[39m
[31m    --source-task-id <TASK_ID> \\[39m
[31m    --block-source \\[39m
[31m    --var SOURCE_FILES=\"<affected source paths>\" \\[39m
[31m    --var TEST_SCRIPT=\"tests/e2e/features/$FEATURE/<failing-test-file>\" \\[39m
[31m    --var TEST_RESULTS=\"tests/e2e/features/$FEATURE/results/latest.md\" \\[39m
[31m    --description \"<root cause and context>\"[39m
[31m  ```·[39m
[31m**If e2e passes or pre-flight skipped**: continue to next iteration (Step 1)·[39m
[31m### Step 4: Continue Loop·[39m
[31mReturn to Step 1.·[39m
[31m## Error Handling·[39m
[31m| Situation | Action |[39m
[31m|-----------|--------|[39m
[31m| No available task | End loop, print summary |[39m
[31m| Agent timeout | Mark blocked, continue next |[39m
[31m| Record missing | Dispatch `Agent(prompt=\"Fix record for task <TASK_ID>\")` — subagent calls `forge prompt get-by-task-id --fix-record-missed` internally |[39m
[31m| 3 consecutive failures | STOP dispatcher |[39m
[31m| Breaking task tests fail (3a) | `forge task add --template fix-task --block-source`, continue loop |[39m
[31m| Feature e2e tests fail (3b) | `forge task add --template fix-task --block-source`, continue loop |[39m
[31m| Main session task fails | Follow error handling in task document's `### Error Handling` section; if missing, `forge task add --template fix-task --block-source`, continue loop |·[39m
[31m## Post-Completion·[39m
[31mAfter all tasks are completed (loop ends with \"No available task\"):·[39m
[31m```[39m
[31mPrint summary to user:[39m
[31m\"All tasks completed. T-test-3, T-test-4, and T-test-4.5 in the task chain handle[39m
[31me2e verification, graduation, and regression automatically.\"[39m
[31m```·[39m
[31mIf the feature's task index does not include T-test-3/T-test-4, suggest:[39m
[31m```[39m
[31m\"Run `/run-e2e-tests` to verify against PRD acceptance criteria,[39m
[31mthen `/graduate-tests` to migrate scripts to the regression suite.\"[39m
[31m```·[39m
[31mDo NOT run e2e tests outside of the Breaking Task Gate (Step 3) — the dispatcher only executes tests as quality gates.·[39m
[31m## Related Commands·[39m
[31m| Command | Usage |[39m
[31m|---------|-------|[39m
[31m| `/execute-task` | Manual single task |[39m
[31m| `/record-task` | Create record + update status |[39m
[31m| `/run-e2e-tests` | E2E verification against PRD |[39m
[31m"[39m
